question: What is the role of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS)?

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat during non-LOCA events when normal cooling is unavailable, ensuring the reactor transitions safely to shutdown without external power. The system maintains the reactor coolant system (RCS) temperature below 420°F within 36 hours, protecting fuel and pressure boundaries, and its reliability and risk significance are evaluated through established programs. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of steel and nickel-based alloys used, their protective claddings, and relevant material standards, ensuring corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. References to applicable sections and standards from the BPVC are also provided.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

edges.description: Section A describes the DHRS's function to remove decay heat and ensure safe shutdown, which operates in conjunction with the RCS described in Section B as the primary coolant circulation system, indicating that the DHRS's role is to support the RCS in maintaining safe reactor conditions during specific events.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of the pressurizer's materials and components, which are part of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and are referenced in Section B's general summary of the reactor coolant system (RCS) and its integral components.
